topic: Swiss Family Reunification for Unmarried Partners: What Evidence Shows a Durable Relationship?
angle: Explains how unmarried partner cases differ from spouse cases and what practical evidence helps show a durable, marriage-like relationship.
notes_for_drafting: Do not imply unmarried partner cases are equivalent to spouse routes.
Explain that cohabitation helps but is not the only evidential factor.
Avoid sounding as if one document type guarantees approval.
used_title: Can an Unmarried Partner Join You in Switzerland?

topic: Swiss Child Reunification After Long Separation: How to Handle Custody, Consent, and Timing Problems
angle: Covers late or difficult child reunification cases where separation, custody arrangements, or consent issues create higher scrutiny.
notes_for_drafting: Do not reduce the case to age limits alone.
Separate custody evidence from relocation-consent evidence.
Acknowledge that sponsor category can change the timing analysis.
used_title: Can You Bring a Child to Switzerland After Years Apart?

topic: Swiss Family Reunification Refused for Housing or Finances: What the Decision May Really Mean
angle: Analyses refusals that appear formal on the surface but may reflect deeper concerns about affordability, household formation, or sponsor stability.
notes_for_drafting: Do not assume the stated refusal reason is the only real issue.
Avoid suggesting that reapplication is always better than appeal.
Show how housing and finances are assessed differently across routes.
used_title: Housing or Finance Refusal in Swiss Family Reunification

topic: Bringing an Elderly Parent to Switzerland: Why Adult Dependent Relative Cases Are So Hard
angle: Explains the high-threshold dependency test for adult relative cases and why emotional closeness alone is usually not enough.
notes_for_drafting: Do not describe this as a standard parent route.
Distinguish dependency evidence from ordinary family affection.
Explain the difference between EU/EFTA ascendant logic and hardship-style cases.
used_title: Can You Bring an Elderly Parent to Live in Switzerland?

topic: Swiss Fiancé Visa or Family Reunification After Marriage? Choosing the Right Route to Marry in Switzerland
angle: Clarifies when the intended-marriage route is the right starting point and how it differs from applying after an existing marriage.
notes_for_drafting: Do not present the fiancé route as a standalone long-term status.
Keep civil-status procedure separate from immigration permission.
